tools.description:
Get semantic context for formulating queries to the TPC-H sales and supply chain database.

This tool provides comprehensive query context information, including:
- Available tables and their descriptions
- Column names and descriptions for querying
- Measures available for analysis
- Example questions that have been asked before

**IMPORTANT**: You should call this tool at least once at the beginning of a session to understand how to formulate questions correctly.

Use this context to help users formulate questions that will work correctly with the search tool.
tools.name: semantic_context

tools.description:
Search and analyze TPC-H sales and supply chain data including orders, customers, suppliers, parts, and regions.

**CRITICAL INSTRUCTION**: Before using this search tool, you MUST call the `semantic_context` tool at least once to understand how to formulate questions correctly.

Args:
    query: The natural language query text to search for.
tools.name: tursio_search

output.name: semantic_context
output.output:
## Overview

This dataset provides a comprehensive record of sales, customers, orders, products, suppliers, and geographic data to support detailed analysis of sales performance, customer behavior, supplier activity, and product distribution across different regions and market segments. It enables tracking and evaluation of the entire sales and fulfillment process from order placement to delivery and supplier management.

## Tables

- **CUSTOMER**: Records individual order line items, detailing quantities, pricing, discounts, shipping, and supplier information for each product within customer orders, supporting comprehensive sales and fulfillment analysis.
- **LINEITEM**: Stores customer details, including unique identifiers, contact information, market segment, account balance, and links to their nation, supporting customer management and analysis.
- **NATION**: Captures customer purchase transactions, detailing order status, pricing, dates, priorities, and responsible staff, serving as a central record for order management and tracking within the sales process.
- **ORDERS**: Stores detailed information on each product, including unique identifier, manufacturer, brand, type, size, packaging, name, and retail price, supporting inventory management and product catalog operations.
- **PART**: Stores supplier details, including unique identifier, contact information, address, and associated nation, supporting supplier management and linkage to geographic data.
- **PARTSUPP**: Stores unique identifiers and names for countries or regions, supporting geographic classification and relationships within the database.
- **REGION**: Stores information about geographic regions, assigning each a unique identifier, name, and descriptive comment for use in linking nations and supporting regional analysis.
- **SUPPLIER**: Links suppliers and parts, detailing available quantity, supply cost, and related comments for each supplier-part combination to support inventory and procurement management.

## Columns

- **Region Key**: Unique identifier for each region
- **Region Name**: Name of the region
- **Region Comment**: Additional comments or notes about the region
- **Part Supplier Key**: Unique identifier for the supplier
- **Part Supplier Available Quantity**: Quantity of the part available from this supplier
- **Part Supplier Part Key**: Unique identifier for the part
- **Part Supplier Supply Cost**: Cost charged by the supplier for this part
- **Part Supplier Comment**: Additional comments or notes about the part-supplier relationship
- **Customer Address**: The address of the customer
- **Customer Custkey**: A unique identifier for the customer
- **Customer Phone**: The phone number of the customer
- **Customer Nationkey**: The key representing the nation associated with the customer
- **Customer Mktsegment**: The market segment to which the customer belongs
- **Customer Acctbal**: The account balance of the customer
- **Customer Name**: The name of the customer
- **Nation Key**: Unique identifier for each nation
- **Nation Name**: Name of the nation
- **Nation Region Key**: Identifier for the region to which the nation belongs
- **Line Commit Date**: The date the order was committed to ship
- **Line Ship Instruction**: Instructions for shipping the item
- **Line Receipt Date**: The date the item is expected to be received
- **Line Tax**: The tax rate applied to the line item
- **Line Line Status**: The current status of the line item
- **Line Line Number**: The line number within the order
- **Line Quantity**: The quantity of the item ordered
- **Line Part Key**: The unique identifier for the part
- **Line Ship Date**: The date the item is scheduled to ship
- **Line Discount**: The discount rate applied to the line item
- **Line Ship Mode**: The mode of shipment for the item
- **Line Return Flag**: Flag indicating if the item was returned
- **Line Supplier Key**: The unique identifier for the supplier
- **Line Order Key**: The unique identifier for the order
- **Line Extended Price**: The total extended price for the line item
- **Order Date**: The date when the order was placed
- **Ship Priority**: The priority level assigned to shipping the order
- **Customer Key**: The unique identifier for the customer who placed the order
- **Total Price**: The total price of the order
- **Order Key**: The unique identifier for the order
- **Clerk**: The name or identifier of the clerk who processed the order
- **Order Priority**: The priority level assigned to the order
- **Order Status**: The current status of the order
- **Supplier Key**: Unique identifier for each supplier 
- **Supplier Name**: Name of the supplier
- **Supplier Phone**: Contact phone number of the supplier
- **Supplier Nation Key**: Identifier for the nation associated with the supplier
- **Supplier Address**: Physical address of the supplier
- **Part Container**: Type of packaging used for the part
- **Part Manufacturer**: Name of the manufacturer of the part
- **Part Size**: Size or dimension of the part
- **Part Part Key**: Unique identifier key for the part
- **Part Brand**: Brand associated with the part
- **Part Name**: Descriptive name of the part
- **Part Retail Price**: Retail price of the part
- **Part Type**: Type or category of the part

## Measures

- **Part Supplier Available Quantity**: 
- **Part Supplier Supply Cost**: 
- **Customer Acctbal**: 
- **Line Tax**: 
- **Line Line Number**: The line number within the order
- **Line Quantity**: 
- **Line Discount**: 
- **Line Extended Price**: 
- **Total Price**: 
- **Part Size**: 
- **Part Retail Price**: 
- **Line Order Key Count**: 
- **Line Line Status Count**: The number of line items grouped by their status in the order line
- **Line Return Flag Count**: The number of line items grouped by their return flag in the order line
- **Customer Custkey Count**: The total number of unique customer keys in the CUSTOMER table
- **Customer Nationkey Count**: The total number of unique nation keys associated with customers in the CUSTOMER table
- **Customer Mktsegment Count**: The total number of unique market segments represented by customers in the CUSTOMER table
- **Order Key Count**: 
- **Order Status Count**: 
- **Part Part Key Count**: 
- **Supplier Key Count**: The total number of unique supplier keys in the table
- **Supplier Nation Key Count**: The total number of unique nation keys associated with suppliers in the table
- **Supplier Count**: Supplier Key
- **Nation Key Count**: 
- **Nation Region Key Count**: 
- **Region Key Count**: 
- **Part Supplier Key Count**: The total number of unique part supplier keys in the PARTSUPP table
- **Part Supplier Part Key Count**: The total number of unique part keys associated with part suppliers in the PARTSUPP table

## Example Questions

- what is the trend of automobile order counts in 2021?
- how was the automobile segment doing in china for 2021?
- which suppliers are offering the most discounts in china?
- Show total line extended price by region name for order date before 2025
- which ones are best positioned to be preferred suppliers?
- Which indian suppliers are shipping the most by air?
- how was the pharma segment doing in india?

summary.text:
**Querying top building segment parts**

I’m figuring out the query for the building segment where CUSTOMER.Mktsegment = 'BUILDING'. I need to compare this across nations, focusing on the likely customer nation. I’m not sure if “parts being ordered the most” refers to quantity or number of line items, so I’ll interpret it by summing line_quantity for each part within the building segment per nation. I’ll aim to get the top 5 parts per nation by total quantity ordered. I’m thinking of using a search tool with natural language for that.

output.arguments: {"query":"For customers in the BUILDING market segment, show for each nation the top 5 parts being ordered, ranked by total LINEITEM quantity. Include nation name, part key, part name, total quantity, and number of distinct orders. Use CUSTOMER->ORDERS->LINEITEM->PART and CUSTOMER->NATION."}
output.name: tursio_search
output.output:
{"sql": "WITH part_nation_stats AS (
  SELECT
    "NATION"."N_NAME" AS Nation_Name,
    "PART"."P_PARTKEY" AS Part_Part_Key,
    "PART"."P_NAME" AS Part_Name,
    SUM("LINEITEM"."L_QUANTITY") AS Total_Quantity,
    COUNT(DISTINCT "LINEITEM"."L_ORDERKEY") AS Distinct_Order_Count,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY "NATION"."N_NAME"
      ORDER BY SUM("LINEITEM"."L_QUANTITY") DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM PUBLIC."LINEITEM" AS "LINEITEM"
  LEFT JOIN PUBLIC."ORDERS" AS "ORDERS"
    ON "LINEITEM"."L_ORDERKEY" = "ORDERS"."O_ORDERKEY"
  LEFT JOIN PUBLIC."CUSTOMER" AS "CUSTOMER"
    ON "ORDERS"."O_CUSTKEY" = "CUSTOMER"."C_CUSTKEY"
  LEFT JOIN PUBLIC."NATION" AS "NATION"
    ON "CUSTOMER"."C_NATIONKEY" = "NATION"."N_NATIONKEY"
  LEFT JOIN PUBLIC."PART" AS "PART"
    ON "LINEITEM"."L_PARTKEY" = "PART"."P_PARTKEY"
  WHERE LOWER(TRIM("CUSTOMER"."C_MKTSEGMENT")) = 'building'
  GROUP BY
    "NATION"."N_NAME",
    "PART"."P_PARTKEY",
    "PART"."P_NAME"
)
SELECT
  Nation_Name,
  Part_Part_Key,
  Part_Name,
  Total_Quantity,
  Distinct_Order_Count
FROM part_nation_stats
WHERE rn <= 5
ORDER BY Nation_Name, Total_Quantity DESC

| VIETNAM        |         1083345 | blanched magenta cream sandy salmon          |              169 |                      4 |", "summary": "**Key Insights**

- For each nation in the BUILDING market segment, the analysis identifies the top 5 parts ranked by total ordered quantity. The displayed dataset provides part details including nation, part key, part name, aggregated quantity, and the count of distinct orders in which each part appears.
  
- Across nations, the total quantities for the top parts range from a low of 155 to a high of 200, while the number of distinct orders per part on average is between 4 and 6.

**Detailed Observations**

- **Nation-Level Ranking:**  
  Each nation is evaluated individually. For instance, in ALGERIA, the top part ("grey floral coral burlywood almond") recorded a total quantity of 181 across 4 orders, followed by parts with total quantities between 165 and 169.

- **Comparison Across Nations:**  
  - In ARGENTINA, top quantities are 194, 177, and so on, with distinct order counts mostly at 4 or 5.  
  - In BRAZIL, the highest quantity is 204 (with 5 orders) and secondary parts range from 173 to 192 in quantity.  
  - European, Middle Eastern, and Asian nations show similar ranges; for example, in FRANCE, quantities range from 163 to 187 while RUSSIA’s best performing part has 194 in total quantity (across 4 orders).  
  
- **Distinct Order Counts:**  
  The number of distinct orders per part typically ranges from 4 to 6 across all nations, indicating consistency in order frequency for top parts within the BUILDING segment.

**Conclusion**

The dataset confidently reveals a nation-by-nation breakdown of the top five parts for the BUILDING segment, with clearly quantified total quantities (ranging from 155 up to 200) and distinct order counts (between 4 and 6). These insights provide an accurate, aggregated view of ordering patterns by nation and part within the specified market segment."}

summary.text:
**Creating a comparison table**

I need to respond in a way that's easy to understand. The data already has 1000 rows, but it focuses on 25 nations with 5 items each, so I'll consider summarizing the top parts for each nation. Looking at the numbers, Brazil leads with 204, followed by Morocco at 200, and the US at 199. I think I can present a concise table showing the top item for each nation and mention that the full top 5 is available for anyone interested. It’s a challenge, but I’ll make it work!

**Compiling a parts comparison**

I plan to present the top part for each nation along with quantities and mention that the full top 5 is available. However, I wonder if just showing the top item will seem inadequate, so maybe I could include the top 3, but that would still be quite a bit of information. The user asked to compare which parts are most ordered in the building segment, so I’ll ensure to highlight maximum quantities and provide SQL queries for clarity. Let's compute the top part based on the first row for each nation!

**Extracting and comparing part quantities**

I’ll extract the quantities for parts from various nations, like Algeria at 181, Brazil with 204, and Morocco at 200. Then, I can compare these quantities to find that Brazil is on top, followed closely by Morocco and the US. I should mention that the part names are based on random color words in TPC-H and include both part keys and names. To wrap it up neatly, I'll create two tables: one for the top part per nation and another for nations with the highest quantities. Let's make this clear and informative!

content.text:
Below is a nation-by-nation comparison of the **most-ordered part** (ranked by **total ordered quantity = SUM(LINEITEM.L_QUANTITY)**) for **customers in the BUILDING market segment**. “Nation” here is the **customer’s nation** (CUSTOMER → NATION).

### #1 most-ordered part in BUILDING segment, by nation (customer nation)

| Nation | Top part (key) | Part name | Total quantity | Distinct orders |
|---|---:|---|---:|---:|
| ALGERIA | 1855045 | grey floral coral burlywood almond | 181 | 4 |
| ARGENTINA | 10903 | black violet pale seashell bisque | 194 | 6 |
| BRAZIL | 1270818 | floral dim aquamarine tomato rosy | 204 | 5 |
| CANADA | 1694820 | royal goldenrod almond yellow brown | 164 | 4 |
| CHINA | 72233 | pale beige rosy hot moccasin | 192 | 5 |
| EGYPT | 1467127 | salmon red royal yellow brown | 184 | 4 |
| ETHIOPIA | 1293160 | rosy mint magenta aquamarine steel | 195 | 5 |
| FRANCE | 458247 | cream navy moccasin sandy honeydew | 187 | 5 |
| GERMANY | 1519455 | steel smoke lawn salmon green | 190 | 5 |
| INDIA | 912045 | snow dim firebrick burnished midnight | 186 | 5 |
| INDONESIA | 808214 | linen puff orange lavender cornflower | 184 | 4 |
| IRAN | 1940644 | spring snow cornflower magenta smoke | 175 | 5 |
| IRAQ | 470253 | pink smoke beige blue seashell | 191 | 5 |
| JAPAN | 536209 | violet drab dim smoke salmon | 183 | 5 |
| JORDAN | 1683161 | violet navy royal lime cyan | 191 | 5 |
| KENYA | 152083 | steel papaya coral slate floral | 177 | 4 |
| MOROCCO | 1674698 | lemon tan sienna deep brown | 200 | 5 |
| MOZAMBIQUE | 462882 | lavender misty orchid spring firebrick | 180 | 4 |
| PERU | 1915888 | orchid misty beige powder brown | 188 | 4 |
| ROMANIA | 524604 | maroon moccasin blue navajo slate | 170 | 4 |
| RUSSIA | 336269 | orchid lawn navajo chartreuse chocolate | 194 | 4 |
| SAUDI ARABIA | 1256466 | snow salmon almond lemon blanched | 191 | 6 |
| UNITED KINGDOM | 871134 | lace azure cream navajo olive | 170 | 4 |
| UNITED STATES | 867880 | sandy pale ivory black steel | 199 | 5 |
| VIETNAM | 524159 | papaya salmon medium peru seashell | 173 | 4 |

### Cross-nation comparison (who has the “biggest” top part?)
Ranking nations by the **quantity of their #1 part**:
- **BRAZIL**: 204
- **MOROCCO**: 200
- **UNITED STATES**: 199
- **ETHIOPIA**: 195
- **ARGENTINA / RUSSIA**: 194 (tie)
- **CHINA**: 192
- **IRAQ / JORDAN / SAUDI ARABIA**: 191 (tie)

If you want, I can also return the **top 5 parts per nation** (it’s 125 rows total) or switch the “nation” definition to **supplier nation** instead of **customer nation**.
